page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, indicating a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The lack of bundled local storage and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represent key architectural tradeoffs, positioning this instance as a compute-focused resource rather than a memory-intensive or storage-heavy node.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed offer predictable compute performance for workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The resource density makes it suitable for lightweight compute-bound tasks, small-scale web serving, microservices, and basic utility processing where dedicated CPU execution is prioritized over high memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6462C processor operating at 3.3 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and does not include any local storage. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Lacking local storage and high memory capacity, this instance represents a tradeoff optimized for CPU-bound tasks. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated, high-frequency CPU performance without large memory demands, such as lightweight web serving, microservices, and small-scale compute utilities.
